_note: Mahabharata character arcs — 7 episodes per character, played sequentially. The script generator walks arcs in order: arc 1 episode 1, 2, ... 7, then arc 2 episode 1, ... etc. State tracking via recent_topics.json (already excludes used topics). Once an arc is fully exhausted (all 7 episodes used in recent_topics), the next arc starts. Designed to train YouTube's Suggested algorithm to pair sequential episodes — channel's RELATED_VIDEO traffic was 0.1% before this system.
_added: 2026-05-15 — channel-focus refactor after 13-day analytics showed topic-salad (random Mahabharata stories per upload) wasn't building Suggested traffic. Sequential character arcs in playlist + episode-numbered titles fix that.
_tier2_2026-05-16: Tier 2 plan review: (a) reordered Bhishma/Karna/Draupadi arcs so each leads with its most emotionally devastating episode (first-impression effect drives playlist sub-conversion). Episode strings unchanged — already-used topics in recent_topics.json still match by topic-string. (b) Added Eklavya (devotion + caste injustice) and Ashwatthama (cursed immortal) — user-identified as naturally viral emotional archetypes. Total arcs: 5 → 7. Total topics: 35 → 49.

arcs.name: Bhishma — The Vow That Doomed a Dynasty
topics: Bhishma's vow of celibacy — the day prince Devavrata chose his father's love over the throne, taking the terrible oath that earned him the name 'Bhishma'
Bhishma raising the Pandavas and Kauravas together — the grandfather torn between two warring factions of his own bloodline
Bhishma's silence during Draupadi's vastraharan — why the greatest warrior of his age could not lift his sword to stop the queen's humiliation
Bhishma in the Kurukshetra war — fighting for the side he knew was wrong, killing soldiers from the family he raised
Shikhandi and Bhishma — the secret promise that revealed the invincible warrior's only weakness
Bhishma's final teachings to Yudhishthira — the Shanti Parva wisdom delivered from a dying bed of arrows
Bhishma on the bed of arrows — 58 days of waiting for death, choosing the moment of his own departure

arcs.name: Karna — The Forgotten Son
topics: Karna's rejection at Draupadi's swayamvara — the wound that defined his life when she called him sutaputra
Karna's birth and abandonment — Kunti's secret child, the son of Surya placed in a basket on the Ganges
Karna's friendship with Duryodhana — the loyalty that bound him to the wrong side of dharma forever
Karna and Parashurama — the curse from a guru who learned his student's true caste at the wrong moment
Karna's gift of kavach and kundal to Indra — the sacrifice of his own protective armor he was born with
Karna's meeting with Kunti before the war — the mother revealing she abandoned him, asking him to spare her sons
Karna's death by Arjuna's arrow — the moment his chariot wheel sank into the earth and the curse came true

arcs.name: Arjuna — The Reluctant Warrior
topics: Arjuna and Drona's bird-eye test — the moment Drona knew this student would become the greatest archer
Arjuna's exile in disguise as Brihannala — the warrior who became a dance teacher to fulfill a vow
Arjuna and Krishna in the Gita — the chariot stops, the cousin becomes the philosopher
Arjuna's vow to kill Jayadratha before sundown — the impossible promise that nearly broke him
Arjuna fighting his own grandfather Bhishma — using Shikhandi as a shield to do what he could never do otherwise
Arjuna fighting his guru Drona — and the lie 'Ashwatthama is dead' that broke the master's spirit
Arjuna's grief after Abhimanyu's death — the father's vow that turned the war on day fourteen

arcs.name: Draupadi — The Queen Who Lit the War
topics: Draupadi's vastraharan in the Kuru sabha — the queen pulled by her hair, the elders silent
Draupadi born from the sacrificial fire — the daughter Drupada asked for to destroy Drona
Draupadi's swayamvara — the moment she chose Arjuna (in disguise) and rejected Karna
Draupadi as wife to five Pandavas — the unusual marriage that bound the brothers forever
Draupadi's vow about her hair — never tying it back until washed in Dushasana's blood
Draupadi in the forest exile — twelve years of waiting, refusing to forget the humiliation
Draupadi's grief after the war — winning a kingdom of corpses, losing all five sons in one night

arcs.name: Yudhishthira — The Cost of Truth
topics: Yudhishthira and the Yaksha at the lake — the questions that defined dharma in the forest
Yudhishthira's gambling addiction — how the truthful king lost everything including his wife
Yudhishthira's lie 'Ashwatthama is dead' — the only lie of his life, and what it cost him
Yudhishthira's refusal to abandon a dog at heaven's gate — the final test of dharma
Yudhishthira's vision of his brothers in hell — the moment heaven tested his attachment
Yudhishthira's rule as king — the prosperous kingdom built on guilt over his cousins' deaths
Yudhishthira's final journey to the Himalayas — walking with his brothers and Draupadi toward death

arcs.name: Eklavya — The Devotion That Cost a Thumb
topics: Eklavya's gurudakshina — the thumb he cut off without hesitation when his guru asked for it, the price of a teacher who had never once taught him
Eklavya practicing before a statue of Drona — the forest student who became a greater archer than Arjuna by training with mud-clay alone
Drona's first refusal — turning away the tribal prince at the gates because his caste did not match the royal academy
The day Arjuna saw a dog with seven arrows in its mouth — the arrows that did not let the dog bark, fired by an archer Arjuna had never met
Eklavya as the king of the Nishadhas — the warrior whose people still remembered his devotion long after the kshatriyas forgot his name
Eklavya's death by Krishna's hand — the dharma-defender Krishna had to kill so Arjuna could remain the greatest archer at Kurukshetra
Eklavya's place in the Bhagavad Gita's silence — the warrior the Gita never named, whose absence haunts every chapter on devotion

arcs.name: Ashwatthama — The Cursed One
topics: Ashwatthama and the lie 'Ashwatthama is dead' — the moment Yudhishthira's only lie killed his father Drona from grief, not blade
Ashwatthama's massacre of the sleeping Pandava sons — the night a son of a Brahmin became a butcher in the Pandava camp
Ashwatthama's Brahmastra against Uttara's womb — the unborn Parikshit attacked by a weapon that should never have been turned on a child
Krishna's curse on Ashwatthama — three thousand years of wandering with a wound on his forehead that would never close
Ashwatthama's gem and the wound — the jewel torn from his forehead that left an exposed scar for the rest of eternity
Ashwatthama still walking the earth — the immortal warrior some say is still seen near Indian forests asking for oil for his wound
Ashwatthama's tragedy as a parable — the man who paid the highest price of the Mahabharata war, beyond death itself
